title: Program Coordinator

rationales.taskAutomation: Scheduling, tracker updates, meeting-minute distribution, and status-deck compilation make up most of a program coordinator's week, and AI now handles that reliably; only escalation judgment and stakeholder follow-through resist full automation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI scheduling assistants, meeting-note tools, and project-tracker automations (Asana AI, Notion AI, Calendly AI) are mature, widely shipped, and already the default in offices that use these platforms.
rationales.adoption: Adoption is spreading fast beyond tech into professional services, healthcare administration, and nonprofits wherever a PMO already runs on Asana, Monday, or Smartsheet.
rationales.agenticExposure: A program coordinator's core loop, track a task, chase a status, update a tracker, remind a stakeholder, is close to an agent spec; what remains is judgment about which chases actually matter.

tasksAtRisk: Document and logistics coordination for program events
tasksGrowing: Escalation judgment on which follow-ups matter
Onboarding and process documentation for new programs
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-1082 Project Management Specialists (junior/coordinator-level), anchored toward the lower end of the p25-p75 range

defenseSkills.skill: AI Scheduling & Tracker Automation (Asana AI, Notion AI)
Agentic AI Literacy for Program Operations

insight: Most of what fills a program coordinator's day, scheduling, tracker updates, minute-taking, is now a background agent task, not a job. Coordinators who move into judgment work, deciding which fire actually needs a human, are the ones who keep a seat.
